posts.title: From OpenClaw to Claude Code: Migrating My AI Assistant Setup to Anthropic
posts.excerpt: I spent months building a personal AI assistant with OpenClaw. cron jobs,  Telegram integration, smart home control, the works. Then Claude Code shipped  Channels and I migrated the whole thing in a weekend. Here's how it went.

posts.title: "Making Sure" Is the Hardest Kind of Doing
posts.excerpt: After 20+ years managing tech product teams, I still struggle to explain what I actually do. The answer always starts with "making sure" — and that never felt like enough. Until I unpacked what it really means.

posts.title: My Homelab Setup: Three Locations, 20+ Services, Zero Cloud Dependencies
posts.excerpt: I run a multi-site homelab across three locations in Portugal, all connected via Tailscale and managed entirely as code with Ansible. Here's the full breakdown of hardware, services, and lessons learned.

posts.title: Syncing Obsidian Tasks with Apple Reminders... The Hard Way
posts.excerpt: I wanted my Obsidian tasks to show up on my watch. Sounds simple, right? It wasn't. Here's how I built a bidirectional sync between Obsidian and Apple Reminders.

posts.title: Are developers missing the mark on using AI?
posts.excerpt: When used correctly, AI can augment a developer's capacity by orders of magnitude. But here's the problem: many developers aren’t using AI correctly.

posts.title: Most usefull AI enhanced tools for productivity and development.
posts.excerpt: I use AI daily, not only when writting code or more broadly working on product development, but also on almost everything I do while dealing with devices
